phases.title: Terminator Safety
phases.goal: Build verified process inspection and termination utility

phases.title: Session API Integration
phases.goal: Add async termination APIs and route callers through them

tasks.title: Process-tree terminator tests
tasks.action: Add tests for missing identity skip, PID start identity mismatch, POSIX missing/mismatched cwd skip, Windows taskkill.exe /PID without shell, Windows taskkill failure reporting, POSIX leaf-first fallback when PGID ownership is not verified, no process-group kill from root PGID alone, verified POSIX force termination after the graceful wait, and surviving sampled child reporting after root exit.
dod: POSIX missing or mismatched cwd skips termination
Windows taskkill failures report failed instead of throwing
POSIX never process-group kills without stronger ownership proof
sampled children that survive root exit are reported as degraded or unverified

test_cases.test_symbol: ProcessTreeTerminator skips termination without root identity
ProcessTreeTerminator rejects PID start identity mismatch
ProcessTreeTerminator skips POSIX termination when cwd is unavailable
ProcessTreeTerminator skips POSIX termination when cwd mismatches
ProcessTreeTerminator uses Windows taskkill by PID without shell
ProcessTreeTerminator reports failed Windows taskkill without throwing
ProcessTreeTerminator avoids POSIX process-group kill when PGID is unverified
ProcessTreeTerminator does not use POSIX process-group kill with root PGID alone
ProcessTreeTerminator force-kills only verified remaining POSIX root
ProcessTreeTerminator reports sampled child that survives root exit

tasks.title: Process-tree terminator implementation
tasks.action: Implement DefaultProcessTreeTerminator, default process info providers, start identity capture, safe Windows/POSIX termination decisions, non-throwing termination failure results, and POSIX leaf-first enforcement until stronger process-group ownership verification exists.
dod: enforce mode validates OS identity before termination
ordinary termination failures return failed telemetry
broad process-name and unverified process-group kills are absent
tasks.rollback: Keep processCleanup.mode = observe and stop calling the terminator from enforce mode.

tasks.title: Async session termination tests
tasks.action: Add tests proving terminateSession awaits enforce cleanup, terminator failures still finalize with failed telemetry, direct delete pre-delete persistence is awaited, tab/workspace/restart paths await async termination after durable lifecycle saves, and batch APIs report missing/all-session results deterministically.
dod: route and WorkspaceService cleanup paths do not finalize sessions before durable lifecycle markers are persisted
ordinary terminator failures do not break delete compatibility
batch APIs report attempted, terminated, and missing ids

test_cases.test_symbol: SessionManager terminateSession awaits enforce process-tree termination
SessionManager terminateSession finalizes when enforce terminator throws
sessionRoutes direct delete does not terminate session when pre-delete workspace save fails
SessionManager terminateMultipleSessions reports mixed missing sessions
SessionManager terminateAllSessions reports deterministic batch result

tasks.title: Async API implementation
tasks.action: Add async termination APIs, preserve deleteSession compatibility, and route direct session/tab/workspace/restart cleanup through awaited termination calls.
dod: batch termination reports attempted, terminated, and missing ids
tasks.rollback: Call legacy deleteSession/deleteMultipleSessions from compatibility paths and keep enforce mode disabled.

tasks.title: Final verification and SRS evidence
tasks.action: Run full server tests, focused Wave0 tests, daemon process-info tests, frontend typecheck, SpecKiwi validation, and independent sub-agent review.
dod: handoff no longer reports Wave2 as absent
tasks.rollback: Mark FR-BGSTAB-011 back to planned if verification fails.

tdd.exempt_reason: Evidence registration and review do not introduce runtime code.

risks.mitigation: Never kill by process name or shell-built command; use verified PID identity only.
Treat missing or mismatched identity as skipped-unverified.
Preserve deleteSession wrapper and HTTP status behavior for compatibility.
Keep shutdown recoverability and policy out of Wave2.
